trainsOnDataNuance: Consumer accounts (Free, Plus, Pro) have data training enabled by default. API platforms, ChatGPT Team, and ChatGPT Enterprise accounts are excluded from training by default.
optOutHow: Through ChatGPT Settings -> Data Controls -> toggle off 'Improve the model for everyone'. Alternatively, non-logged-in users can submit a request form on the OpenAI Privacy Center.
retentionPeriod: If chat history is enabled, data is stored indefinitely. If chat history is disabled, conversations are retained for 30 days to monitor for abuse before deletion.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Users can delete individual chats or their entire account. Account deletion requests are typically processed within 30 days, although backup copies may persist shortly thereafter.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with vendors, service providers, and affiliates for operational purposes, security monitoring, and legal compliance. Not sold to third-party advertisers.
humanReviewConditions: A sample of user chats may be reviewed by human trainers to improve model safety and performance, unless the user has opted out of training or is using enterprise/API services.
regionalVariation: EEA, UK, and Swiss users are subject to a specific privacy policy. They have access to GDPR rights (e.g. right to object) and can request data deletion or opt-out via dedicated regional compliance workflows.
childrenDataPolicy: Minimum age is 13 (or regional equivalent). Under 18 requires parental or guardian consent. OpenAI does not knowingly collect data from children under 13.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Enterprise (Team & Enterprise tiers) and API keys feature zero data training by default, custom retention agreements, and compliance certifications (SOC 2, GDPR).

trainsOnDataNuance: Consumer chats (Claude.ai Free and Pro) are used for model training by default (effective late 2025). Console API and Enterprise plans are excluded from training by default.
optOutHow: Through Claude.ai Settings -> Data Privacy Controls -> toggle off 'Help improve Claude'. Users can also request opt-out through the Privacy Center.
retentionPeriod: If training is disabled, data is retained for 30 days. If training is enabled, de-identified conversational data may be retained for up to 5 years for research and model development.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Chats can be deleted from the active UI. Account deletion requests are typically completed within 30 days.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with hosting providers (AWS, Google Cloud) and service providers under strict data privacy agreements. No sharing with advertisers.
humanReviewConditions: Safety reviews are performed when conversations are flagged for potential abuse or terms of service violations. Otherwise, human reviews are minimized for opted-out users.
regionalVariation: GDPR baseline applies for EU/UK users. Anthropic has designated EU and UK representatives to handle data protection inquiries.
childrenDataPolicy: Strictly limits services to individuals 18 years of age or older. No child data is knowingly processed.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Console API and Claude Enterprise accounts have data training disabled by default, custom data retention options, and HIPAA/SOC 2 compliance options.

trainsOnDataNuance: Consumer Gemini apps (web and mobile) train on chats if Gemini Apps Activity is turned on (on by default). Google Workspace and Google Cloud (Vertex AI) do not train on user data.
optOutHow: Go to myactivity.google.com -> Gemini Apps Activity -> turn off the activity toggle. Note: opting out of training also disables chat history on the platform.
retentionPeriod: Default activity retention is 18 months (adjustable to 3 or 36 months). When activity is turned off, chats are cached for 72 hours for service delivery. Human-reviewed/annotated chats are stripped of user info and kept for up to 3 years.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Users can delete individual prompt threads or clear all Gemini activity. However, previously reviewed/annotated data that has been disconnected from the Google account cannot be deleted.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with internal reviewers and contractors for annotation. Google does not sell personal conversational data to third parties.
humanReviewConditions: Conversations are sampled and reviewed by human annotators to improve Google's AI technologies. These samples are disconnected from the user's account before review.
regionalVariation: EEA/UK users have access to GDPR rights. In some regions, Gemini Apps Activity settings have different default prompts or require explicit consent.
childrenDataPolicy: Available to users 13+ (or regional age of consent). Teens have specific default settings, and Google applies strict safety filters for teen interactions.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Google Workspace Enterprise, Vertex AI, and Google Cloud customers are covered under enterprise agreements ensuring zero data training, zero human review of chats, and local residency support.

trainsOnDataNuance: Meta AI trains on your interactions with the AI. Additionally, Meta trains its models on all public posts, photos, and comments on Facebook and Instagram by default.
optOutHow: EU/UK users can opt out via the 'Right to Object' form in the Privacy Center under GDPR. For US and most other non-EU users, there is no general setting to opt out of AI training on public posts, but setting accounts to private restricts training data to some extent.
retentionPeriod: Vague. Conversations and posts are retained for as long as Meta deems necessary to train and improve its AI models or provide services.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Users can delete Meta AI chat threads. However, public posts that have already been ingested into model training weights cannot be deleted or recalled from the model.
thirdPartySharing: Shared across Meta platforms and with external partners and service providers. Not sold directly to brokers, but used to target personalized advertisements.
humanReviewConditions: Conversations with Meta AI may be reviewed by human moderators for safety compliance, policy enforcement, and model tuning.
regionalVariation: EU and UK users have legally protected rights to object to AI training, which Meta must honor. US, LATAM, and APAC users have no equivalent general opt-out rights.
childrenDataPolicy: Services are for users 13+. Meta does not knowingly target AI products directly at children under 13, but public posts of teen users may be harvested.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Meta does not offer a hosted enterprise AI service with the same dedicated B2B privacy guarantees as OpenAI or Microsoft, though Llama models run on third-party enterprise platforms (like Azure or AWS) under those platforms' specific terms.

trainsOnDataNuance: Consumer Copilot (free and Pro) uses conversations for training by default. Enterprise/school accounts with commercial data protection (Enterprise Data Protection, EDP) are completely excluded from training.
optOutHow: In consumer Copilot settings, navigate to Privacy -> Model Training and toggle off 'Model Training for Text/Voice'.
retentionPeriod: Consumer conversations are kept in the account history. If history is disabled, Microsoft retains Copilot data for 30 days for abuse monitoring before permanent deletion.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Users can delete chat history from the Copilot UI or manage it comprehensively via the Microsoft Privacy Dashboard (account.microsoft.com/privacy).
thirdPartySharing: Shared with service providers and Microsoft affiliates. Copilot query and conversation data is shared with OpenAI under strict confidentiality terms for model execution, but OpenAI cannot train on Microsoft user data.
humanReviewConditions: Subject to human review during trust and safety audits, abuse reports, or compliance checks. Flagged logs may be held up to 30 days for review.
regionalVariation: EEA/UK users have access to GDPR privacy dashboards and enhanced telemetry opt-out options.
childrenDataPolicy: Requires a Microsoft account, which restricts users under 13 (or regional age limit) without explicit parental verification.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Copilot for Microsoft 365 and Copilot with Enterprise Data Protection (EDP) enforce zero model training, zero human review of chats, and all data is encrypted at rest and in transit.

trainsOnDataNuance: Chats on the consumer 'Le Chat' interface are used for model training by default. API platform usage and enterprise subscriptions exclude model training by default.
optOutHow: In Le Chat settings, open the Data & Account Controls panel and uncheck the 'Enable data sharing' checkbox.
retentionPeriod: Conversations are saved to the account history indefinitely until deleted by the user. If opted out, inputs/outputs are not collected for training.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Users can delete chats directly in the interface. Deletion is instantaneous in the active system; backup copies are cleared in accordance with their standard backup retention cycles.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with cloud infrastructure hosts (such as Scaleway, Azure, and AWS) in compliance with European privacy standards.
humanReviewConditions: Human review of chats is highly restricted, limited to instances of abuse reports, security incidents, or when legally mandated.
regionalVariation: Based in France; the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) serves as the baseline for all users globally, ensuring strong default rights.
childrenDataPolicy: The consumer Le Chat service is restricted to users who are 18 years of age or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: The developer API platform does not train on query data by default. Enterprise contracts offer formal GDPR clauses and dedicated deployment capabilities.

trainsOnDataNuance: Consumer queries are used for model training by default. Perplexity Enterprise Pro accounts are automatically excluded from model training.
optOutHow: In Account Settings, toggle off the 'AI Data Retention' option. Note: Opting out only prevents future queries from being trained on; it does not retroactively remove data already used.
retentionPeriod: Searches and thread history are stored until deleted by the user. Retained for training purposes unless opted out.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Users can delete individual search threads or request full account deletion via account settings or support email.
thirdPartySharing: Queries may be sent to third-party LLM providers (e.g. OpenAI, Anthropic) to generate answers, but under contracts that prohibit those providers from using Perplexity data for model training.
humanReviewConditions: Conversations may be processed for safety monitoring and performance evaluation, occasionally involving human review of flagged queries.
regionalVariation: Fully compliant with GDPR for EEA users, providing tools to export data, request deletion, and restrict processing.
childrenDataPolicy: Strictly limited to users 13+ (or 16+ in the European Economic Area). Underage accounts are deleted upon discovery.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Perplexity Enterprise Pro features single-sign-on (SSO), data encryption, zero data training by default, and immediate data deletion upon workspace deletion.

trainsOnDataNuance: Both your direct chats with Grok and your public posts on the X (Twitter) platform are used by default to train Grok models. API platform accounts do not train on data.
optOutHow: On the X platform (web or app), go to Settings and Privacy -> Privacy and safety -> Grok, and toggle off 'Allow your posts as well as your interactions, inputs, and results with Grok to be used for training'. On the x.ai portal, go to Data Controls and deselect 'Improve the model'.
retentionPeriod: Conversations and posts are retained as long as necessary for model training and service delivery.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Grok chat history can be cleared in the UI. However, public posts on X that have already been ingested into training sets cannot be easily purged or recalled.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with X Corp affiliates (such as Tesla) and service providers under joint-venture or vendor agreements.
humanReviewConditions: Logs may undergo human review for safety filtering, alignment verification, and reinforcement learning purposes.
regionalVariation: GDPR controls are available to EU/UK users. Subject to strict scrutiny by the Irish Data Protection Commission (DPC) regarding Twitter post ingestion.
childrenDataPolicy: Age limit is 13+ (aligned with X platform registration terms).
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Developer API platform services have separate terms where prompt inputs are not used for training and are subject to standard enterprise security policies.

trainsOnDataNuance: Free consumer chats on DeepSeek Chat are used to train and optimize DeepSeek models by default. API platform usage is excluded from training by default.
optOutHow: In consumer settings (DeepSeek Terms of Use), users can opt out of the model training program by turning off the 'Improve the model for everyone' toggle.
retentionPeriod: Retained for the duration necessary to achieve the business purposes described in the policy. De-identified or aggregated data may be retained indefinitely.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Users can delete chats from their history or request full account deletion. Deletion requests are processed in accordance with local regulations (such as CAC in China and GDPR in Europe).
thirdPartySharing: Shared with affiliates, infrastructure providers, and authorities under Chinese cybersecurity laws and international compliance rules.
humanReviewConditions: Conversations are monitored and may be reviewed by human annotators to ensure compliance with Chinese safety laws (CAC) and model optimization.
regionalVariation: Vast difference between domestic Chinese CAC regulation compliance and international privacy policies for overseas users. Overseas users have data hosted outside mainland China (e.g. in Singapore).
childrenDataPolicy: Strictly limited to users 18 years of age or older. Accounts of underage users will be terminated upon discovery.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: DeepSeek API platform usage does not train on query data by default, offers temporary data caching (up to 30 days) solely for abuse detection, and complies with standard enterprise security expectations.

trainsOnDataNuance: AWS Bedrock does NOT use customer data (inputs, outputs, or custom model weights) to train or improve any base models by default. Other AWS AI services (like Polly or Rekognition) might collect data by default but allow account-level opt-out.
optOutHow: Opt-out is the default for Bedrock (no action needed). For other AWS AI services, you can opt out via AWS Organizations by attaching an 'AI services opt-out policy'.
retentionPeriod: No conversational data is stored by AWS for Bedrock inference. Customer data remains within their selected AWS region VPC.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Customers have full ownership and control over their data, which can be deleted instantly through standard AWS console or API commands.
thirdPartySharing: AWS does not share customer prompts or outputs with model providers (such as Anthropic, Meta, or Cohere) or any other third parties.
humanReviewConditions: There is zero human review of prompts or responses processed through Amazon Bedrock.
regionalVariation: Compliance is managed at the AWS region level (e.g., EU-West-1 for GDPR alignment, US-East-1 for US compliance).
childrenDataPolicy: AWS is a cloud developer platform restricted to users 18 years of age or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Bedrock is an enterprise developer service with comprehensive B2B compliance (HIPAA, SOC, GDPR), data encryption keys managed by the customer (KMS), and VPC connectivity.
